title: Parthasarathy Temple, Mundakkayam
tagline: Parthasarathy Temple, Mundakkayam — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Sri Parthasarathy Temple is a Hindu temple located in Mundakkayam town in Kanjirappally taluk of Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala.
overview.description: Sri Parthasarathy Temple is a Hindu temple located in Mundakkayam town in Kanjirappally taluk of Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is situated on the road to Kuttikkanam, about 52 km from Kottayam and 20 km from Kuttikkanam.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Parathodu
topPlaces.description: Parathodu is a village under Kanjirappally Taluk in the Kottayam district of Kerala, India. It is located 44 km east of Kottayam, in the eastern midland–highrange belt, the area has an economy largely supported by rubber and other plantation crops.

topPlaces.name: Parathanam
topPlaces.description: Parathanam is a town at the base of Western Ghats mountain ranges in the Kottayam district, Kerala. It is 2,000 feet (610 m) above sea level and is situated on the eastern border of Kottayam District, 60 km from Kottayam, 12 km away from Poonjar and around 8 km away from Mundakayam on the NH 220 (Kottayam–Kumily Road).

topPlaces.name: Koottickal
topPlaces.description: Koottickal is a town at the base of Western Ghats mountain ranges in the Kottayam district, in the state of Kerala. It is 2,000 feet (610 m) above sea level and is situated on the eastern border of Kottayam District, 55 km from Kottayam, and around 5 km away from Mundakayam on the NH 220 (Kottayam–Kumily Road).

topPlaces.name: Koorali
topPlaces.description: Koorali is a place situated at Kottayam district of Kerala, India. Panamattom, Elamgulam, Thampalakad, Kanjirappally and Ponkunnam are the nearest places to Koorali.

topPlaces.name: Pathampuzha
topPlaces.description: Pathampuzha is a small village in the Kottayam district of Kerala state, India. Aruvikkachal waterfalls is one of the tourist attraction here.

topPlaces.name: Edakkunnam
topPlaces.description: Edakkunnam is a village in Kanjirappally Taluk of Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India. The nearest town is Kanjirappally.

topPlaces.name: Kanjirappally
topPlaces.description: Kanjirappally is a taluk and a town in Kottayam district situated about 38 km (24 mi) away from the district capital, in the state of Kerala, in southwestern India.

topPlaces.name: Kokkayar
topPlaces.description: Kokkayar is a village in Azhutha block of Peerumedu taluk in Idukki district, in the Indian state of Kerala. It is surrounded by Koottikkal, Elappara panchayaths in north, Peerumedu and Elappara panchayaths in east, Peruvanthanam in south and Koottikkal, Mundakkayam panchayaths in west.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Parthasarathy Temple, Mundakkayam?
faq.a: The best time to visit Parthasarathy Temple, Mundakkayam is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Parthasarathy Temple, Mundakkayam?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (79 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (74 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Parthasarathy Temple, Mundakkayam?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Parthasarathy Temple, Mundakkayam at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Parthasarathy Temple, Mundakkayam?
faq.a: Stays in Parthasarathy Temple, Mundakkayam start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Parthasarathy Temple, Mundakkayam?
faq.a: The top attractions are Parathodu, Parathanam, Koottickal, Koorali, Pathampuzha.
